hero.sub: DMARC aggregate reports arrive as XML built for machines. Sentinely reads them for you and turns the noise into a clear verdict — plus the controls to act on it.

verdict.alert: Spoofing source detected — fails SPF & DKIM, listed on 1 blocklist
verdict.caption: Live verdict for a monitored domain

signals.heading: Everything that decides if your domain is trusted
signals.sub: Sentinely watches the full stack of signals mailbox providers use to trust — or reject — your mail.

problem.heading: DMARC holds the evidence — in a format built for machines.
problem.sub: The reports that could tell you who's abusing your domain arrive as raw XML. Almost nobody reads them, so three things quietly go wrong.

cards.title: Impersonation goes unseen
cards.body: Anyone can send as your domain. Without DMARC visibility, spoofing and phishing run silent — until a customer or your brand gets burned.

cards.title: Legit mail gets blocked
cards.body: A missing SPF include or a broken DKIM key quietly sends your real mail to spam, and the reports that explain why sit unread.

cards.title: You never reach enforcement
cards.body: Staying at p=none feels safe, but it leaves the door open. Moving to reject without data is scary — so most domains never do.

features.heading: A defense, not a data dump.
features.sub: Sentinely turns raw reports into decisions: a score to track, a digest to read, and alerts that reach you before anyone else notices.

cards.title: Domain protection score
cards.body: A single 0–100 grade (A–F) that rolls SPF, DKIM, DMARC and alignment into one number you can watch over time.

cards.title: Daily digest + AI Insights
cards.body: One readable email a day. Claude explains what changed and what to do about it — in English or Italian.

cards.title: Threat & change alerts
cards.body: Get told the moment a new source starts spoofing you, authentication drops, or a critical record changes.

cards.title: DNS & auth hardening
cards.body: Guided checks for SPF, DKIM, DMARC, BIMI and MTA-STS — with the exact records to publish, copy-ready.

cards.title: Blocklist & reputation
cards.body: Continuous RBL/DNSBL, Geo-IP and PTR/rDNS monitoring, so a listing or bad sender never catches you off guard.

cards.title: Built for many domains
cards.body: Multi-tenant from day one. Manage dozens of domains and clients from one workspace, cleanly separated.

how.heading: One record in. Control out.
how.sub: No agents, no gateway changes, no mail rerouting. You publish a single DMARC record and Sentinely does the reading.

steps.title: Publish your RUA
steps.body: Add one DMARC record pointing aggregate reports to Sentinely. That's the only change on your side.

steps.title: We ingest & analyse
steps.body: We parse every aggregate report, resolve each source, and check authentication, alignment and blocklists — every day.

steps.title: Take back control
steps.body: Read the verdict, fix what's weak, and move safely from p=none to enforcement (p=reject) with the data to back it.

digest.heading: The one email that reads the reports for you.
digest.sub: Every morning, a digest that summarises what happened and tells you what matters — with AI Insights that explain the why, not just the numbers.
digest.insightBody: A new source, 193.42.33.8, sent 604 messages as acme.com and failed both SPF and DKIM. It's listed on one blocklist and matches none of your known senders — this looks like spoofing, not lost legitimate mail. No action needed on your side; keeping DMARC at p=reject ensures these are rejected.

pricing.heading: Start free. Scale by domains, not surprises.
pricing.sub: A 14-day free trial on every plan. No card to start.
pricing.footnote: Prices in EUR, excl. VAT. Introductory pricing — subject to confirmation.

starter.name: Starter
starter.tagline: For a single domain getting to enforcement.

growth.name: Growth
growth.tagline: For teams protecting a handful of domains.

agency.name: Agency
agency.tagline: For MSPs and agencies running many tenants.

items.q: Do I need to be a DMARC expert?
items.a: No. Sentinely translates raw reports into plain-language verdicts and tells you exactly which record to publish. If you can edit a DNS entry, you're set.

items.q: Will it change my email or DNS?
items.a: No. Sentinely is read-only: we only receive the DMARC reports you route to us. We recommend the records to publish, but you always make the change yourself.

items.q: How quickly will I see results?
items.a: As soon as your RUA points to us, reports start flowing. Most domains get their first full digest within 24 hours.

items.q: Can I manage multiple domains or clients?
items.a: Yes. Sentinely is multi-tenant by design — ideal for IT and security teams and for agencies or MSPs running email for many domains.

items.q: Where is my data stored, and for how long?
items.a: DMARC report data is processed and stored in the EU and retained on a rolling window. See our GDPR/DPA page for the specifics.
The interface, alerts, daily digest and AI Insights are all available in both English and Italian.

finalCta.heading: See who's sending as your domain.
finalCta.sub: Start free — publish one DNS record and you'll have your first verdict within a day.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how Purple IT s.r.l. ("Sentinely", "we") processes personal data when you visit this website, contact us, or use the Sentinely service.

sections.heading: Who we are
sections.body: Sentinely is a product of Purple IT s.r.l., Perugia (PG), Italy, VAT IT01881930661. We are the data controller for the personal data described here.

sections.heading: What we collect
sections.body: Website analytics kept to a minimum, the details you submit through our contact and affiliate forms, and — for customers — account data and the DMARC aggregate reports routed to the service.

sections.heading: Why we process it
sections.body: To answer your enquiries, provide and secure the service, comply with legal obligations, and communicate about your account. DMARC report data is processed to deliver the analysis, scoring and alerts you ask for.

sections.heading: Your rights
sections.body: You may request access, rectification, erasure, restriction or portability of your personal data, and object to certain processing. Contact info@sentinely.eu to exercise these rights.

sections.heading: Retention & location
sections.body: Data is processed and stored in the EU and retained no longer than necessary for the purposes above. See our GDPR/DPA page for processing details.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms govern access to and use of the Sentinely website and service, provided by Purple IT s.r.l. By using Sentinely you agree to these Terms.

sections.heading: The service
sections.body: Sentinely ingests DMARC aggregate reports and provides analysis, a protection score, alerts and a daily digest. Sentinely is a read-only analysis tool and does not send, filter or reroute your email.

sections.heading: Accounts
sections.body: You are responsible for the accuracy of the information you provide and for maintaining the security of your account and credentials.

sections.heading: Acceptable use
sections.body: You agree to use Sentinely only for domains you are authorised to manage, and not to misuse, disrupt or attempt to gain unauthorised access to the service.

sections.heading: Plans & billing
sections.body: Paid plans are billed as described at sign-up. Pricing shown on this site is indicative and may change before general availability.

sections.heading: Liability
sections.body: The service is provided "as is". To the extent permitted by law, Purple IT s.r.l. limits its liability as set out in the final Terms published before go-live.

gdpr.title: GDPR & Data Processing
gdpr.intro: This page describes how Sentinely, a product of Purple IT s.r.l., approaches the GDPR and acts as a data processor for customer data, including DMARC aggregate reports.

sections.heading: Roles
sections.body: For customer account and report data processed on your behalf, Purple IT s.r.l. acts as data processor and you as data controller. For this website and our own communications, we are the controller.

sections.heading: Data residency
sections.body: Report data and account data are processed and stored within the European Union.

sections.heading: Sub-processors
sections.body: We use a limited set of vetted sub-processors (for hosting, email delivery and AI-generated insights). A current list is available on request and will be published here before go-live.

sections.heading: Data Processing Agreement
sections.body: A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is available for customers. Request a copy at info@sentinely.eu.

sections.heading: Security
sections.body: We apply appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect personal data. Details are provided in the DPA and final documentation.

about.heading: Email authentication, made legible.
about.sub: Sentinely exists because the evidence to stop spoofing already arrives in your inbox every day — trapped in XML nobody reads. We turn it into something a person can act on.

sections.heading: What we do
sections.body: We ingest DMARC aggregate reports, resolve every sending source, check authentication and reputation, and turn the result into a clear verdict, a protection score, smart alerts and a daily digest with AI Insights.

sections.heading: Who it's for
sections.body: IT and security teams protecting their own domains, and the agencies and MSPs who protect email for many clients at once. Multi-tenant, EU-hosted, bilingual.
